narrative: Noon on Sunday and downtown is busier than it should be. The community meeting about the proposed development on North Broadway drew sixty people to the public library, which is forty more than the organizers expected. Petra Szabo is there because the project intersects with her firm’s West End work, and she stands up and says something precise about drainage and sight lines that shifts the conversation from emotional to technical. Amber is in the back row, arms crossed, listening with the evaluating expression of someone who has opinions she’s still organizing. The meeting runs hot — a man in a Carhartt jacket says the word ‘gentrification’ three times and a woman from the planning office keeps saying ‘stakeholder input’ like a prayer. Up on the Rimrocks overlook, Ryan and his kids are eating sandwiches and looking at the city spread below them, snow patchy on the rooftops. Cody wants to know where exactly the mountain lion was. Leah tells him mountain lions don’t attack people in groups, which is true and also not entirely reassuring.
events: A community meeting about downtown development gets heated at the public library — Petra attends and speaks; Amber is in the audience
Ryan takes his kids to the Rimrocks overlook (the open part, not the closed trail) and Cody asks about the mountain lion eleven times
